task: Implemented macOS voice notification system for askr init to enable users to opt-in to spoken state updates via native `say` command, integrated with existing Discord notification hooks to announce task completion and session state transitions; fixed handover generation bug leaking .claude/ directory noise into uncommitted files list; diagnosed and fixed get_state_dir() fallback vulnerability that could load a different project's stored path, and scrubbed foreign-repo (leaps) contamination from shared state files; reproduced and documented nested worktree cwd-drift lockout as a genuine but collision-specific bug requiring manual escape via absolute paths; identified project root storage as potential fix for nested worktree lockout but deferred implementation pending security review of askr_state/.gitignore exposure.
discussion_summary: Session focused on deliberately reproducing the nested worktree lockout condition to confirm it is a genuine bug, not a phantom collision artifact. Successfully created a test worktree at .claude/worktrees/repro-test and confirmed that cd into the nested worktree's askr_state directory blocks normal navigation (cd .., pwd, ls all fail with permission/path errors) but can be escaped via absolute paths (/bin/pwd) or relative traversal (cd ./../../..). User proposed storing absolute project root during askr init as a fix, which is sound in principle but requires careful handling of askr_state/.gitignore exposure and cross-repo contamination risk before implementation. No code changes were made this session; all work was diagnostic and confirmatory.

accomplishments.what: Scoped voice notification feature: confirmed macOS-only deployment enables native `say` TTS without cross-platform abstraction
Identified notification hook integration points (askr/hooks/stop.py, notification.py) for wiring voice updates as additional sink alongside Discord
Designed voice notification preference schema: machine-level boolean flag stored in global ~/.config/askr/config.json (not per-project)
Implemented load_voice_enabled() and save_voice_enabled() helper functions in askr/state/config.py following existing config pattern
Diagnosed handover generation bug: _get_uncommitted_files() in checkpoint.py dumping raw git status output without .claude/ filtering
Fixed checkpoint.py to exclude .claude/ directory from uncommitted files list in handover documents
Verified no active nested worktree lockout condition at session start; prior session's guard collision was event-specific, not standing bug
Deliberately reproduced nested worktree cwd-drift lockout by creating test worktree and confirming cd/pwd/ls fail when cwd is inside nested worktree's askr_state
Confirmed nested worktree lockout can be escaped via absolute paths (/bin/pwd) or relative traversal (cd ./../../..)
Discovered critical security issue: get_state_dir() could fall back to loading a different project's stored path from ~/.config/askr/config.json if current project had no stored path
Fixed get_state_dir() to always return the current project's state directory without cross-project fallback contamination
Scrubbed 7 lines of foreign-repo (leaps) contamination from askr_state/failed_approaches.md that had leaked into shared state tracking
Updated project memory (project_handover_repo_scoping.md and MEMORY.md) to reflect corrected understanding of get_state_dir() bug as root cause, not symptom

Evaluated user proposal to store absolute project root during askr init as fix for nested worktree lockout; identified security concern: askr_state/ is .gitignored but storing absolute paths there risks cross-repo contamination if .gitignore is bypassed or state directory is shared

in_progress.what: Wire voice_enabled preference through askr init flow and integrate voice notification sink alongside Discord webhook in task completion flow

Design and implement nested worktree lockout fix: store absolute project root in a safe location (candidate: .claude/settings.json or environment variable set by askr init) to allow get_state_dir() to validate cwd against project root and escape lockout via absolute path fallback

next_actions.action: Complete voice notification integration in askr/hooks/stop.py: wire load_voice_enabled() call into task completion flow and emit `say` command with task summary if voice_enabled is true
next_actions.why: Voice notification feature is scoped and designed but not yet wired into the actual notification pipeline; this is the final integration step

next_actions.action: Design nested worktree lockout fix: decide whether to store absolute project root in .claude/settings.json (already tracked, not .gitignored) or via environment variable set by askr init, then implement get_state_dir() validation logic to detect cwd drift and fall back to absolute path
next_actions.why: User identified a sound fix approach; implementation requires careful placement to avoid cross-repo contamination and .gitignore bypass risks

next_actions.action: Test nested worktree lockout fix by creating a test worktree, entering it, and confirming that get_state_dir() now returns correct path and cwd navigation works without manual escape
next_actions.why: Reproducible bug must be verified fixed before closing; test worktree setup is already documented

next_actions.action: Review and commit all pending changes: voice notification implementation, nested worktree fix, and any related test updates
next_actions.why: Current session has only diagnostic work; next session should land the actual fixes

decisions.decision: Voice notifications are macOS-only feature, no cross-platform abstraction required
decisions.reason: askr init runs on macOS; native `say` command is available and sufficient; cross-platform TTS adds complexity without user demand

decisions.decision: Voice notification preference stored at machine level (~/.config/askr/config.json), not per-project
decisions.reason: User preference for spoken updates is a machine-wide setting, not project-specific; aligns with existing config pattern

decisions.decision: Nested worktree lockout is a genuine bug, not a phantom collision artifact, and should be fixed
decisions.reason: Reproducible with clean test setup (no sub-agent interference); blocks normal shell navigation in nested worktrees; escape via absolute paths confirms it is a cwd-validation issue, not a transient state

decisions.decision: Nested worktree lockout fix deferred pending implementation design review
decisions.reason: User proposed storing absolute project root; sound in principle but requires careful placement to avoid cross-repo contamination and .gitignore bypass risks; design must be reviewed before coding

relational_files.why: Notification sink that will receive voice_enabled preference and emit `say` command alongside Discord webhook
Candidate location for storing absolute project root to fix nested worktree lockout; already tracked and not .gitignored
Per-project state file; get_state_dir() fallback vulnerability was rooted in cross-project config loading from this file
askr_state/ is .gitignored; nested worktree fix must not store absolute paths in .gitignored directories to avoid cross-repo contamination
